goals.description: Finish the reading response draft in my room, then immediately collapse onto the bed for sleep.
goals.rationale: I can't risk Roommate_R asking about my absence tomorrow morning; finishing this quickly lets me get away from them and finally rest. If I leave it unfinished, the guilt of their disappointment will keep me awake anyway.

goals.description: Send a low-effort text to the cohort group saying 'Just caught up on some reading, back soon!' so I don't feel left out tomorrow.
goals.rationale: My brain is fried and trying to engage deeply feels like too much effort. Just dropping a quick message keeps me connected without requiring me to actually go outside or deal with the noise. It's safer than leaving silently and avoids the awkwardness of being ignored by my friends.

goals.description: Do absolutely nothing about the smell or mess until after I've finished the draft and gone to bed.
goals.rationale: Fixing the room feels like admitting failure to roommate_R, and I can't bear the thought of them seeing me struggling while they're happy. Plus, dealing with the mess now will drain the energy I desperately need to write. I'll just pretend nothing happened tonight; the guilt comes later anyway, but it's better to focus on finishing the work first.
